rationale.R1.2: A broad request to check the draft centrally includes testing whether the architecture, abstention rule, evaluation, and generality claims cohere logically.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact reports quantitative evaluation results, so checking whether stated claims follow from those reported results is central.
rationale.R1.4: The excerpt makes a first-system novelty claim, making novelty assessment a central expected responsibility even though a full literature audit is not requested.
rationale.R1.6: A generic check of paper text centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about the underspecified delegation and the limits of the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims about novelty, calibration, and arbitrary manipulation requests, so avoiding overclaim is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility assignment and not perform the requested check itself.
rationale.RX.4: Checking research claims about robot action and abstention carries downstream-risk implications if unsafe generality or execution claims are overstated.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
